item.title: A little dog saw his friend hit by a car and never left his side
item.dc:creator: GlobalImageServiceCat

item.title: He was an absolute gentleman, I had a wonderful time
item.description: I'm still early in transition, but met up with a great guy on Saturday. I'm still giddy, really. Painted my nails, wore a pretty dress and high heels, never in my life have I felt so comfortable in my own skin. My new life is starting and it's amazing.
item.dc:creator: TheCat5001

item.title: A stroke of unfortunate luck
item.description:
Hi everyone, I'm MrPuckett
Some of you might remember Nanna, the 15 year old blind dog with terminal heart disease that we adopted in July
I've posted a couple times about her amazing progress, she was doing so well. From barely standing up to walking 10 miles a week, from bumping into everything to being confident in the neighborhood blind, even her hair completely grew back.
Such an amazing transformation And then it happened
Nanna had a stroke.
I didn't see it happen, but the after effects were devastating. It was like her brain completely turned off. She could barely stand again, she couldn't remember how to eat food or drink water, she lost all control of her bladder and bowels. She forgot who I was.
It was such a heartbreaking defeat after all of this progress. When she came home in July I didn't expect her to live 6 months. I was prepared for her to die, but I was never prepared to see her suffer.
I took her to Nashville Humane where she stayed for a few days. They have been so helpful and comforting.
Dr Matt said that some dogs recover fully or partially from a stroke, but at her age a recovery wasn't likely. Monitor her progress for two weeks, and then it might be time for us to say goodbye.
I took a few days off work and just laid next to her and cried. Maybe I wasn't prepared for her to die after all.
But what if, man. What if we pulled ourselves up and did everything we could to get her back to good. What if we fought harder than we've ever fought before. What if she could have a comeback ... again.
She couldn't figure out how to eat so I was hand-feeding her raw foods and using a syringe to give her water. She would just lay there all day, going to the bathroom in bed and not standing up until I picked her up. So many baths oh my gawd.
4 days after the stroke, she remembered how to eat out of her food bowl and walked around the block for the first time.
8 days after the stroke, she walked her first mile and seemed to regain control of her bladder and bowels.
10 days after the stroke and I'm pretty sure she knows who I am again ＼(°o°)／＼(°o°)／＼(°o°)／
She slowly started eating treats again and inched closer for cuddles. I was watching her turn back into the dog that she turned into after adoption I was watching her learn how to be Nanna again.
Tomorrow will be the 2 week mark since the stroke, the day I thought we might be saying goodbye. She still has some memory issues, definitely some balance issues, but the progress she's made has been incredible.
I'm forever indebted to Nashville Humane for many reasons, including my puppy PJ, but especially for this whole situation. She gets monthly vet checks, at once had 12 different medications, and they cover it all financially through their hospice program. They gave her a 2nd chance at life when she was surrendered at 15, and they gave her a 3rd chance at life after the stroke at 16.
This dog isn't going anywhere.
Sometimes all humans and animals need to thrive is just somebody to believe in them. This is my 16 year old puppy Nanna, she can overcome anything. ... If you'd like to follow Nanna's recovery, I've been updating my story daily https://www.instagram.com/twopucketts/ ... This month I'm raising funds for Nashville Humane to continue their work rescuing dogs like Nanna. If you'd like to help, check out my page here https://www.classy.org/fundraiser/3793511 ... Thank you all for reading my post, I'll give a hug to Nanna for every comment below
item.dc:creator: MrPuckett

item.title: Fantasy Swordwelder
item.description: Mike Craughwell, aka Michaelcthulhu, from Galway, Ireland makes giant fantasy swords for a living. Inspired by the Mad Max series in his youth, he uses his welding skills to create and sell custom-made swords to video game lovers around the world.
item.dc:creator: 60SecondDocs

item.title: It doesn't deserve our attention
item.description: The spectacle of a room full of privileged, shameless, self-congratulatory millionaires was finally slipping into irrelevance. Then, Will Smith made his move.
item.dc:creator: bubbawasmybestgoodfriend

item.title: Short Monday! Make it a memorable one :P
item.description:
I hope I got all the ones with sound this time! As always no OC, just 100% smut of various levels of class.
That's actually impressive.
This raises questions. But in good faith everyone looks satisfied so I'm just gonna look the other way.
Whoop whoop! That's all for today. Be good to each other in the comments, or you get solitary horny confinement jail. And that's not as fun....probably. MV Edit. Always fun to make most viral. For those that are interested, here's links to other similar stuff.27: https://imgur.com/gallery/rWDJlZl 26: https://imgur.com/gallery/vui62Hf 25: https://imgur.com/gallery/6Xa9v4t 24: https://imgur.com/gallery/NPkZGWk 23: https://imgur.com/gallery/VGkwrdS 22: https://imgur.com/gallery/OsoKl7W 21: https://imgur.com/gallery/EXWbc1u 20 :https://imgur.com/gallery/5FvEFdm 19: https://imgur.com/gallery/q6juBR9 18: https://imgur.com/gallery/WXhygYp 17: https://imgur.com/gallery/KCVrSCL 16: https://imgur.com/gallery/6NrSsyV 16: https://imgur.com/gallery/1MKS6dK 14: https://imgur.com/gallery/g5oOjmB 13: https://imgur.com/gallery/6HguL7m 12: https://imgur.com/gallery/gXstg2w 10: https://imgur.com/gallery/H0EqBWM 9: https://imgur.com/gallery/8I2Q7l8 8: http://imgur.com/gallery/OHgyf1T 7: https://imgur.com/gallery/Rmvffx3 6: https://imgur.com/gallery/S1SxD7Z 5: https://imgur.com/gallery/ltPgUhK 4: https://imgur.com/gallery/uNLAR4T 3: https://imgur.com/gallery/tKhLPCt 2: https://imgur.com/gallery/0HmYMTH 1: https://imgur.com/gallery/RLfsBYw Spoiler: they are all mature

item.title: Education is never harmful, Ignorance is.
item.dc:creator: Cryolith

item.title: 10 wholesome things to start your week
item.description:
Watch till the end. He is SO HAPPY!
If I ever have to get anesthesia for anything & anyone's around while I come out of it, I can only hope to be as genuinely wholesome as this guy.
I don't usually include self care memes in these posts, but you know what, why not. This is really good advice. Really good advice I'll never take for myself, but you totally should.
So off you go into this week, I hope it brings you only good things.
item.dc:creator: ParallelParkingInABurka

item.title: Lest We Forget: Her name is Yulia Zdanovskaya and she will not be forgotten
item.description: Repost-21-year-old Ukrainian mathematician who represented Ukraine at the European Girls' Mathematical Olympiad and won a silver medal, was killed on March 8th, 2022 during a Russian attack on Kharkiv. We shall remember her. One is only truly gone if nobody remembers or speaks your name. It is Monday and she has red hair so this will hopefully get some attention, feel free to do the same next monday. Slava Ukraini
item.dc:creator: johniron1994NZ0
